commands.review_summary:
This PR sets the cached ACP runtime handle's applied control signature during session initialization and adds a regression test so startup model/thinking options are not resent as config controls on the first turn.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +72. Total +74 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: `sessions_spawn` can pass startup `timeoutSeconds`, `ensureSession` cannot receive it, and the new cache signature would make the first `runTurn` skip applying it as a config control.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs a contributor-visible code correction and real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not queue repair markers while the external real-proof gate is still unmet.

target.title: fix: avoid reapplying ACP startup runtime options
target.body:
## Summary
- mark runtime options supplied during ACP session startup as already applied for the cached runtime handle
- keep persisted runtime options intact for future resume/recovery
- add a regression covering an OpenCode-style startup model/thinking override that must not be resent as generic config controls on the first turn

## Why
OpenClaw can pass `model` and `thinking` into `ensureSession` at ACP startup, which is the right path for adapters that require startup-time model selection. The cached runtime state previously did not record that those controls were already applied, so the first turn tried to resend them through `session/set_config_option`. OpenCode ACP does not support that generic post-start model control, causing managed OpenCode sessions to fail even though direct OpenCode/acpx works.

## Test
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/acp/control-plane/manager.initialize-session.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.runtime-config.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/runtime-options.test.ts`
target.author: moeedahmed
